why_this_action.summary: The highest-leverage action available in this run was to repair the loop's false-positive publisher-contact lane so it stops counting non-sendable targets as actionable work during a flat-adoption measurement window.
supporting_reasons: Codeberg and GitHub are both flat in the recent measurement window, so fake progress needs to be cut quickly.
Recent directory and curator bursts are already in active measurement windows, limiting legitimate same-family external moves.
The remaining untouched primary-repo-flat publisher target (ctxt.dev / Signum) only exposed website/Telegram paths in the shared discovery artifact.
This runtime already proved Telegram cross-context delivery is blocked, so treating that target as executable would keep regenerating unsendable packet work.
A safe internal repair path existed in the selector/executor, and the marketing contract explicitly says to apply it in the same run when it improves real outcomes.
shared_findings_used: adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg remains flat, so action selection quality matters more than more artifacts
primary_repo_flat_contact_discovery_latest.json: ctxt.dev / Signum was the remaining untouched publisher target
marketing_2026-05-24_ctxtdev_channel_ready_outreach.json: target had already been converted into a ready asset without a sendable runtime path
marketing_2026-05-24_primary_repo_flat_contact_manual_delivery.json: manual-delivery style follow-through already existed for this lane
reddit_post_analysis.json: Reddit is still degraded/blocked and not the best same-run lane

result.summary: Patched the marketing selector/executor to count only runtime-executable publisher channels for the primary-repo-flat lane, added regression tests, and verified the live selector now holds instead of resurfacing an unsendable publisher packet.
